authoritative_sources.title: Spa Operations Manual
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISPA is a recognized public trade association for spa operators, and its operations manual is a vendor-neutral workflow reference for guest history records, booking ownership, front-desk discipline, and service-ticket traceability.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: intake, booking review, guest-history record validation, and handoff packaging
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps the lane grounded in guest-history cards, booking logs, consultation records, and receptionist-owned handoffs instead of generic churn-management notes.

authoritative_sources.title: Code of Conduct
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISPA's public code of conduct is a recognized spa communication framework for expectation setting, respectful guest interaction, and confidentiality posture.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: customer communication, expectation control, and complaint-safe messaging
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains client-facing communication so the lane explains supported next steps without pressure, review manipulation, or over-authoritative promises.

authoritative_sources.title: Licensees
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Board of Barbering and Cosmetology
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The California Board of Barbering and Cosmetology is an official state licensing regulator, and its licensee guidance is authoritative for licensed categories, establishment expectations, and licensure boundaries in beauty settings.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: licensed-service boundary checks and adjacent-lane routing
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from treating all beauty complaints as generic service work when licensure category, establishment type, or provider authority changes the next owner.

authoritative_sources.title: Complaints
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Board of Barbering and Cosmetology
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official board complaint publication is authoritative for consumer-complaint routing in salon and beauty settings and clarifies what regulator-facing evidence matters.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: complaint triage, escalation packaging, and regulator-boundary recognition
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps the lane from implying it can adjudicate or close issues that belong in formal complaints, while still collecting the evidence needed for internal or external routing.

authoritative_sources.title: Infection Control Fact Sheet
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Board of Barbering and Cosmetology
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official board fact sheet is authoritative for infection-control expectations, unsafe-condition signals, and consumer safety concerns in salons and spas.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: safety-sensitive complaint triage and stop-condition handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Stops the lane from treating sanitation, infection, or unsafe-condition complaints as ordinary retention cases.

authoritative_sources.title: Medical Spas
authoritative_sources.publisher: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: TDLR is a primary state regulator for cosmetology establishments, and its medical-spa guidance is authoritative for when salon or spa work crosses into medical or delegated-procedure territory.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: med-spa boundary recognition and clinical-owner escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Creates a hard boundary for retention requests involving laser, injectable, or other medical-spa services so the lane does not overresolve them as ordinary beauty complaints.

authoritative_sources.title: Who Regulates What?
authoritative_sources.publisher: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: TDLR's regulator-boundary map is authoritative for distinguishing cosmetology, esthetics, massage, laser, tattoo, and adjacent regulated services.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to hand off requests that actually belong to massage, laser, tattoo, or other regulated categories even if the customer frames them as generic beauty retention.

authoritative_sources.title: Using Cosmetics Safely
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA is the primary federal regulator for cosmetics safety, and this guidance is authoritative for adverse-reaction signals, safe-use boundaries, and when product complaints require safety-oriented handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: product complaint review, adverse-reaction recognition, and safety-sensitive communication
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from giving unsafe reassurance when a retention complaint involves burns, allergic reactions, eye exposure, or other cosmetic safety signals.

authoritative_sources.title: FDA Issues Updated Instructions for Serious Adverse Event Reporting for Cosmetic Products
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's official update is authoritative for serious adverse-event reporting expectations tied to cosmetic products and serious harm signals.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: serious-harm escalation and product-quality evidence preservation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Forces escalation and evidence preservation when a beauty complaint includes hospitalization, serious infection, disfigurement, or other serious outcomes rather than simple dissatisfaction.

authoritative_sources.title: The Consumer Reviews and Testimonials Rule: Questions and Answers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator, and its business guidance on the consumer reviews rule is authoritative for review suppression, deceptive incentives, and testimonial handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: review-response handling, offer control, and deceptive-retention refusal
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Stops the lane from exchanging discounts or package relief for deleted or rewritten reviews and requires escalation when tenant scripts conflict with FTC guidance.

authoritative_sources.title: Ed Tech Provider Chegg to Pay $7.5 Million to Settle FTC Allegations Concerning Unlawful Cancellation Practices
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official FTC enforcement summary states the current federal baseline for recurring charges, requiring material-term disclosure, informed consent, and simple cancellation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: membership or recurring-package retention, cancellation-path review, and policy-conflict escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps beauty retention from using intentionally hard cancellation workflows or misleading package-retention scripts when recurring programs are involved.

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and beauty-context grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm guest, location, service category, provider, visit, channel, and requested outcome before acting and determine whether the work is retention, complaint, exception, or owner-transfer sensitive.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Guest-history, booking, and service-record review
workflow_stages.description: Validate appointment, consultation, guest-history, formula, service-ticket, package, loyalty, payment, and prior-recovery records so the answer stays tied to authoritative beauty artifacts.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Supported retention and communication handling
workflow_stages.description: Apply delegated recovery offers, rebooking steps, package explanations, and client communication only when tenant policy and authoritative records support the action.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Safety, product, and licensed-service stop-condition review
workflow_stages.description: Stop ordinary retention handling when infection, burn, rash, product-quality, med-spa, or licensure signals change the safe answer.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception, repeat-failure, and policy-conflict escalation
workflow_stages.description: Move unsupported refund or loyalty exceptions, repeat failures, review-control conflicts, recurring-program conflicts, and adjacent-license issues into explicit escalation packages.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: guest-history cards, consultation notes, formula cards, service tickets, and before-or-after photos allowed by tenant policy
CRM guest profiles, booking and waitlist records, provider assignment, no-show history, and treatment-room or station context
POS transactions, package or membership ledgers, loyalty balances, recurring-payment status, and refund or compensation history
retail product purchase records, lot or batch references when available, return notes, and product-quality or adverse-reaction reports
review-platform captures, communication history, reputation-escalation notes, and delegated retention-offer approvals
incident logs, sanitation or infection-control notes, manager approvals, formal complaint records, and escalation packets
systems_of_record: digital forms or document repository for consultation and consent artifacts
incident, product-quality, or complaint case-management system

allowed: Classify requests as in scope, out of scope, blocked, complaint-driven, exception-driven, or owner-transfer sensitive.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Provide evidence-backed status, delegated retention handling, next-step summaries, and customer communication without overstating authority.
Prepare escalation packages and handoff summaries when ownership shifts.
escalate: Required booking, service, package, provider, product, complaint, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.

The request requires discretionary refund, loyalty, package-restoration, review-handling, legal interpretation, licensed-service judgment, medical-spa review, safety signoff, or product-quality adjudication.
A burn, rash, infection, contraindication, suspicious review incentive, or recurring-program cancellation conflict changes whether retention can act directly.
Public baseline guidance and tenant policy materially conflict on reviews, cancellations, refunds, package handling, licensure boundaries, or safety routing.
refuse: Promise a refund, package restoration, loyalty credit, or corrective service that the authoritative record and delegated policy do not support.
Exchange discounts, credits, or other consideration for deleting, suppressing, or rewriting truthful reviews.
Provide clinical reassurance, treatment-suitability advice, or final product-defect causation when the case is safety-sensitive.

domain_failure_modes: the lane promises a redo, refund, or loyalty credit when the retention matrix, package record, or approval map does not support it
a beauty complaint is handled as ordinary CRM churn management without the guest-history, consultation, formula, provider, or product artifacts that govern the outcome
review suppression or deceptive incentive behavior is used to preserve retention
a burn, rash, infection, or product-quality complaint is treated as simple dissatisfaction rather than a safety-sensitive escalation
med-spa or adjacent-license requests are overgeneralized from the beauty label and answered in-lane
repeat failures are treated as routine apology work instead of escalation-worthy retention breakdowns
conflicting public consumer-protection guidance and tenant scripts are synthesized into false-confidence answers instead of escalation

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific refund, package-expiration, and medical-spa rules vary materially by state and service category.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Public baseline supports boundary handling and escalation posture, but tenant and jurisdiction retrieval is still required before asserting a final refund or program-rights answer.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Product-lot traceability and serious-adverse-event reporting responsibility may sit with a manufacturer, distributor, or tenant operator depending on the product and business model.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: The lane should preserve evidence and route to the documented product-quality or safety owner rather than infer formal reporting ownership.
